---
title: "Stats140XP Final Project"
author: "Junjiang Chen"
date: "2026-02-28"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```
```{r}
path <- "C:/Users/junji/OneDrive/Desktop/Sample - Superstore.csv"
store_df <- read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

cat("Rows:", nrow(store_df), "  Cols:", ncol(store_df), "\n")
head(store_df)
names(store_df)
```
```{r}
# Make names consistent with your code:
# - convert spaces to dots so "Order Date" becomes "Order.Date"
names(store_df) <- gsub(" ", ".", names(store_df))
names(store_df) <- gsub("\\.+", ".", names(store_df))  # remove double dots if any

names(store_df)
```
```{r}
store_df <- store_df %>%
  mutate(
    Order.Date = as.Date(Order.Date, format = "%m/%d/%Y"),
    Ship.Date  = as.Date(Ship.Date, format = "%m/%d/%Y")
  )

# Time Variable: 
store_df <- store_df %>%
  mutate(
    Ship.Delay = as.numeric(Ship.Date - Order.Date),
    Order.Year = format(Order.Date, "%Y"),
    Order.Month = format(Order.Date, "%m")
  )

# Removing Useless Columns:
store_df <- store_df %>%
  select(-Row.ID, -Order.ID, -Customer.ID, -Product.ID)

# Creating Profit Margin Feature: 
store_df <- store_df %>%
  mutate(Profit.Margin = Profit / Sales)

# Creating Loss Indicator:
store_df <- store_df %>%
  mutate(Loss = ifelse(Profit < 0, 1, 0))

# Converting Categorical Data To Factors:
store_df <- store_df %>%
  mutate(
    Segment = as.factor(Segment),
    Region = as.factor(Region),
    Category = as.factor(Category),
    Sub.Category = as.factor(Sub.Category),
    Ship.Mode = as.factor(Ship.Mode)
  )
```

```{r}
# Create discounted group: TRUE if Discount > 0
store_df <- store_df %>%
  mutate(
    Discounted = ifelse(Discount > 0, "Yes", "No"),
    Discounted = as.factor(Discounted),
    Profit.Margin = ifelse(!is.na(Sales) & Sales > 0, Profit / Sales, NA_real_)
  )

# Quick count
table(store_df$Discounted, useNA = "ifany")
summary(store_df$Discount)
```

```{r}
profit_summary <- store_df %>%
  group_by(Discounted) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(Profit)),
    mean_profit = mean(Profit, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_profit = sd(Profit, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_profit = median(Profit, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

margin_summary <- store_df %>%
  group_by(Discounted) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(Profit.Margin)),
    mean_margin = mean(Profit.Margin, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_margin = sd(Profit.Margin, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_margin = median(Profit.Margin, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

profit_summary
margin_summary
```
```{r}
ggplot(store_df, aes(x = Discounted, y = Profit)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Profit: Discounted vs Not Discounted",
       x = "Discounted?", y = "Profit")

ggplot(store_df, aes(x = Discounted, y = Profit.Margin)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Profit Margin: Discounted vs Not Discounted",
       x = "Discounted?", y = "Profit Margin")
```
```{r}
# Remove rows with missing values for each test
profit_data <- store_df %>% filter(!is.na(Profit), !is.na(Discounted))
margin_data <- store_df %>% filter(!is.na(Profit.Margin), !is.na(Discounted))

# (1) Two-sample t-test (Welch) for Profit
t_profit <- t.test(Profit ~ Discounted, data = profit_data)
t_profit

# (2) Wilcoxon rank-sum for Profit
w_profit <- wilcox.test(Profit ~ Discounted, data = profit_data, exact = FALSE)
w_profit

# (3) Two-sample t-test (Welch) for Profit Margin
t_margin <- t.test(Profit.Margin ~ Discounted, data = margin_data)
t_margin

# (4) Wilcoxon rank-sum for Profit Margin
w_margin <- wilcox.test(Profit.Margin ~ Discounted, data = margin_data, exact = FALSE)
w_margin
```
